content.Text:
[Thu 2026-04-23 11:52 PDT] Add a "Smart Follow-Up Reminder" feature to this browser-based chat app. The app is a single-page app with index.html, app.js (all modules as IIFEs in revealing-module pattern), and style.css. ## What to build A `FollowUpReminder` module that: 1. Automatically scans conversation messages for action items, commitments, and follow-ups (phrases like "I'll do X", "remind me to", "don't forget to", "TODO:", "need to", "should do", "will check", "follow up on", "let me know", etc.) 2. Extracts the action item text and optionally a time hint (e.g., "tomorrow", "in 2 hours", "next week", "by Friday") 3. Shows detected reminders in a slide-out panel (Alt+Shift+R to toggle) 4. Each reminder shows: the action text, source message excerpt, detected time, status (pending/done/snoozed/dismissed) 5. Users can manually add reminders too 6. Timed reminders trigger browser Notification API alerts (with permission request) 7. Reminders persist in localStorage per session 8. A small badge on the toolbar button shows count of pending reminders 9. Proactive: after each AI response, auto-scan for new action items and show a subtle toast "🔔 New reminder detected" if found ## Implementation details ### app.js Append a new IIFE module `FollowUpReminder` at the end of app.js. Follow the same revealing-module pattern as other modules. Key functions: - `init()` - setup, load from storage, request notification permission, start timer loop - `scanMessage(text, role, messageIndex)` - regex/keyword extraction of action items - `parseTimeHint(text)` - extract relative time references, return Date or null - `addReminder(item)` - add to list, save, update badge - `togglePanel()` - show/hide the reminder panel - `checkDueReminders()` - runs every 30s, fires notifications for due items - `render()` - update panel DOM Hook into ChatController's message flow to auto-scan new messages. ### index.html Add a toolbar button before the closing toolbar div: ```html ``` Add the panel HTML after other panels (search for existing panel patterns like mood-ring-panel or deadline-panel): ```html
``` ### style.css Add styles for: - `#reminder-panel` - slide-out panel matching existing panel styles - `.reminder-item` - individual reminder cards with status colors - `.reminder-badge` / `#reminder-badge` - notification count badge (small red circle) - `.reminder-toast` - subtle notification toast - Status colors: pending=blue, done=green, snoozed=yellow, dismissed=gray - Overdue items get a red left border ## Build verification After making changes, run: `node -c app.js` to verify syntax is valid. ## Important - Do NOT create new files - only modify app.js, index.html, and style.css - Follow existing code patterns exactly (IIFE revealing module, SafeStorage usage, etc.) - The module should be fully self-contained - Use SafeStorage.get/set for persistence (not raw localStorage) - Register keyboard shortcut Alt+Shift+R in the KeyboardShortcuts help modal
I’m inspecting the existing SPA structure first so the reminder feature can fit the current module, panel, storage, and shortcut patterns without fighting the codebase.
`rg` isn’t installed here, so I’m switching to PowerShell text search and reading the existing panel and shortcut sections directly before patching.
